purpose: Builds the reverse map for the Live Preview tab: scans a project's JSX/TSX/HTML files for `data-synapse-label="..."` attributes and produces `{ [label]: { file, line, text } }`. Used by the Preview overlay to resolve a clicked element to its source file location and current text. Caches the map in-memory keyed by projectPath and invalidates when any tracked file's mtime changes or when new files appear.
gotchas: Cache is keyed by projectPath only — switching projects rebuilds the cache, but identical paths from different machines (unlikely) would share state.
Staleness check stat()s every previously-scanned file plus walks the project tree once per call to count files. On large projects (~10k files) getLabelMap() can be hundreds of milliseconds.
If a file is moved/renamed, the next getLabelMap() detects it via the file-count delta and triggers a full rescan — single-file deletes are caught by the stat-fail branch.

Label values are unwrapped through normalizeLabel: removes wrapping `{}`, `()`, `""`, `''`, `` `` `` so JSX-escape-style attributes (data-synapse-label={"abc"}) are normalized to bare 'abc'. Mismatched wrappers produce silently wrong output.

The labelAttrRegex uses a 6-alternative capture group for value forms; ordering matters because regex alternation is left-priority. Any label containing the matching quote character will fail to capture (the regex is non-escaping).
patterns: mtime-based cache invalidation: `cachedMtimes[file] !== fs.statSync(file).mtimeMs` triggers full rescan.
extractTextContent strips JSX expressions and child tags then collapses whitespace — surfaces only the literal text, matching what the user sees in the preview overlay.
Path-relative label entries (`path.relative(projectPath, filePath)`) so the renderer-side overlay can treat them as portable strings.
conventions: Same SKIP_DIRS list as InstrumentService (node_modules, .next, dist, build, .git) — must stay in sync with InstrumentService and PreviewTextWriter.
Returns errors as `{error: 'message'}` for projectPath validation; missing-file conditions inside the scan are silently skipped.
